merged_events.event_description: American Legation established in Prague when Richard Crane presented his credentials as U.S. Minister to Czechoslovakia.

evidence.quote: Richard Crane was appointed U.S. Minister to Czechoslovakia on April 23, 1919. He presented his credentials on June 11, and served until December 5, 1921.

merged_events.event_description: American Legation in Prague closed by Minister Carr following German occupation of Prague.

evidence.quote: Wilbur J. Carr, former Assistant Secretary of State and Chief of the Consular Bureau, was Minister to Czechoslovakia when German forces occupied Prague on March 15, 1939, Carr closed the Legation on March 21, and departed on April 6.

merged_events.event_description: U.S. Minister Biddle presented credentials to the Czechoslovakian Government-in-Exile in London, re-establishing nonresident legation-level relations.

evidence.quote: On September 4, 1941, Anthony J. Drexel Biddle Jr. was appointed Minister to the Czechoslovakian Government in London. He presented his credentials on October 28.

merged_events.event_description: Biddle promoted to Ambassador to the Czechoslovakian Government-in-Exile in London, elevating the nonresident mission to ambassador level.

evidence.quote: Biddle was promoted to Ambassador on June 4, 1943, and presented his new credentials on July 12.

merged_events.event_description: Embassy Prague established with Alfred W. Klieforth as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim, elevating the mission from legation to embassy status.

evidence.quote: Embassy Prague was established on May 29, 1945 with Alfred W. Klieforth as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

merged_events.event_description: Czechoslovakia ceased to exist as a state, ending U.S. diplomatic representation to Czechoslovakia. Succeeded by the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

evidence.quote: On December 31, 1992, the Czech and Slovak Federal Republic ceased to exist and was succeeded by two new states: the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

merged_events.event_description: Anthony J. Drexel Biddle Jr. presented credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to the Czechoslovak government-in-exile, re-establishing a U.S. Legation near the government.

evidence.quote: Anthony Joseph Drexel Jr Biddle, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1941-09-17. Credentials presented: 1941-10-28. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1943-07-12.

merged_events.event_description: Biddle was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and presented credentials on 1943-07-12, elevating the mission to an Embassy near the Government of Czechoslovakia in England.

evidence.quote: Anthony Joseph Drexel Jr Biddle, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1941-09-17. Credentials presented: 1941-10-28. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1943-07-12.

matched.notes: Exact match on date (1919-06-11) and status (Legation). Both refer to Richard Crane presenting credentials in Prague.
Exact match on date (1939-03-21) and status (None). Both refer to Minister Carr closing the Legation after German occupation.
Exact match on date (1945-05-29) and status (Embassy). Both refer to Embassy Prague being established with Klieforth as Chargé d'Affaires ad interim.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Biddle promoted to Ambassador to the Czechoslovakian Government-in-Exile in London, elevating the nonresident mission from Envoy Nonresident to Ambassador Nonresident level. RDCR gives promotion date June 4, 1943; POCOM gives credential presentation date July 12, 1943. Per source priority, RDCR's explicit date of June 4 for the promotion is an appointment/promotion date (not an institutional date), so POCOM's credential date of July 12 may be more appropriate for when the elevation took effect. However, RDCR explicitly states 'promoted to Ambassador on June 4, 1943' which could be read as the institutional elevation date.
missing_from_csv.notes: This event represents an intermediate status change between the 1941 re-establishment of the nonresident legation and the 1945 opening of Embassy Prague. The CSV jumps directly from Legation (1941) to Embassy (1945), missing this ambassador-level nonresident period. Date choice between 1943-06-04 (RDCR promotion) and 1943-07-12 (POCOM credentials) requires judgment.

missing_from_csv.event_description: Czechoslovakia ceased to exist as a state on December 31, 1992, succeeded by the Czech Republic and Slovakia, ending U.S. diplomatic representation to Czechoslovakia.
missing_from_csv.notes: This is the terminal event for U.S.-Czechoslovakia relations. The CSV has no closing event; the last entry is the 1945 Embassy establishment. This should be added as the final row.

unsupported_in_sources.notes: No extracted event covers the establishment of a U.S. Consulate in 1869. Neither the RDCR narrative nor POCOM records mention this event. It may come from other historical sources not included in this extraction pipeline.

No extracted event covers a status change to 'None' on 1917-04-09. This likely refers to the closure of consular relations or a break related to WWI, but neither RDCR nor POCOM extractions mention this event. It may derive from sources outside this pipeline.

discrepancies.extracted_value: RDCR (Event 2): Date 1941-10-28, Status Envoy Nonresident; POCOM (Event 6): Date 1941-10-28, Status Legation
discrepancies.reasoning: The CSV date of 1941-09-04 corresponds to the appointment date mentioned in RDCR ('On September 4, 1941, Anthony J. Drexel Biddle Jr. was appointed Minister'). However, appointment dates are personnel dates, not institutional dates. RDCR does not provide a distinct institutional re-establishment date separate from the personnel dates. Per source priority rules, when RDCR only provides a personnel date (appointment), POCOM's credential presentation date should be used. Both RDCR and POCOM agree on October 28, 1941 as the credential presentation date, which is when the legation relationship was effectively re-established. On status: the CSV says 'Legation' but since Biddle was accredited to the government-in-exile in London (not a resident mission), 'Envoy Nonresident' (from RDCR) is more precise. The recommended correction is date 1941-10-28 with status 'Envoy Nonresident'.
